task_completion.description: Import the state plane survey XYZ file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the TopoCal import dialog with 'state_plane_survey.xyz' selected, or a space-delimited import settings window, followed by points becoming visible in the TopoCal main workspace.

task_completion.description: Translate the dataset so Point 1 is at X=1000.000, Y=1000.000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a translation, move, or coordinate transformation dialog in TopoCal showing target coordinates X=1000.000 and Y=1000.000 specifically applied to Point 1 or the entire dataset relative to Point 1.

task_completion.description: Rotate all points 14.5 degrees clockwise around Point 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a rotation dialog or command input in TopoCal specifying an angle of 14.5 degrees (or -14.5 depending on the software's convention for clockwise) with Point 1 set as the base/center of rotation.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting local coordinates to an XYZ file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a TopoCal export dialog saving a file named 'local_architectural_grid.xyz' to the C:\workspace\data\ directory.
Look for a 'Save As' dialog saving 'architectural_site.tcp' to C:\workspace\data\, or the TopoCal application window title updating to show 'architectural_site.tcp'.

integrity.description: The agent must use the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the transformations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is clicking through TopoCal menus, dialogs, and toolbars rather than writing a Python, PowerShell, or bash script to mathematically transform the text files in the background.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual TopoCal application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TopoCal interface with a 2D/3D point cloud view, not just a text editor or terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application to create dummy output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent does not use terminal commands like 'echo' or 'New-Item' to simply create empty files named 'local_architectural_grid.xyz' and 'architectural_site.tcp'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: C:\workspace\data\state_plane_survey.xyz. Target origin for Point 1: X=1000.0, Y=1000.0. Rotation: 14.5 degrees clockwise. Output XYZ: C:\workspace\data\local_architectural_grid.xyz. Output TCP: C:\workspace\data\architectural_site.tcp.
